title: When Meeting New People: - Focus on Details: Pay Attention to Unique Features, Like
subtitle: Overcome Cross-Race Effect
description:
When meeting new people:
- Focus on details: Pay attention to unique features, like hairstyle, clothing, or mannerisms.
- Make connections: Associate the person with an activity or place to reinforce recognition.
- Practice mindfulness: Avoid rushing interactions—give yourself time to observe.

Example: If you meet someone at a conference, link their face to a topic they discussed.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Count the number of names you can recall within 24 hours after meeting new people. This will serve as your baseline for improvement.

howTo.title: Practice the 10–12 second protocol
howTo.summary: When meeting new people, take 10–12 seconds to observe their unique features and create a contextual tag. Repeat their name aloud or internally.

howTo.title: Log your interactions
howTo.summary: After each interaction, log the name, visual detail, and context tag in the Brali LifeOS app. This reinforces memory and aids recall.

howTo.title: Review and adjust your targets
howTo.summary: At the end of the week, assess your recall success and adjust your targets for the following week to ensure continuous improvement.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your techniques. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/remember-names-and-faces/.
